title: Everything Is Expensive. Treat Yourself to One of These Amazon Prime Day Picks Under $30
gist: WIRED's Prime Day under-$30 deals guide updated June 25 (2747 words, Molly Higgins), retaining the June 24 economic distress framing ('Thirty dollars doesn't get you as much as it used to... as the economy worsens, Buy Now Pay Later services boom, daily bills skyrocket, and inflation continues to surge'). New additions in the June 25 update: Sofolor Interactive Cat Toy ($24), Clocky alarm clock on wheels ($24), Roxicosly vibrating alarm clock ($17), TP-Link Tapo C120 security camera ($30), Urpower essential oil diffuser ($19), and Bentgo Stackable Lunch Box ($12).

themes.theme: Consumer Economic Distress Framing Persists Across Prime Day Updates

insights.claim: WIRED's June 25 update of the under-$30 Prime Day guide retained identical economic distress framing from June 24: 'Thirty dollars doesn't get you as much as it used to. In times like these, as the economy worsens, Buy Now, Pay Later services boom, daily bills skyrocket, and inflation continues to surge'—the language was unchanged across both versions.
insights.speaker: Molly Higgins
insights.so_what: The persistence of explicit economic distress language across two successive WIRED Prime Day guide updates—unchanged from June 24 to June 25—indicates this framing is deliberate editorial positioning, not a one-off, strengthening it as a qualitative consumer sentiment signal for mid-2026.
insights.quote: Thirty dollars doesn't get you as much as it used to. In times like these, as the economy worsens, "Buy Now, Pay Later" services boom, daily bills skyrocket, and inflation continues to surge, it's more important than ever to make that dollar stretch.

themes.theme: June 25 New Additions: Sleep Tech and Smart Home Accessories

insights.claim: Clocky alarm clock on wheels ($24, 11% off from $27) was added to the June 25 update—an alarm clock that propels itself and rolls away when the alarm sounds, forcing the user to physically get out of bed to turn it off—described as 'one of the most successful alarms I tested because it made me physically move.'
insights.speaker: Molly Higgins
insights.so_what: Clocky's inclusion in a WIRED deals guide reflects growing editorial attention to sleep and wakefulness tech as a consumer category; physical behavioral design (rolling away) as a sleep intervention differentiates it from app-based sleep products.
insights.quote: one of the most successful alarms I tested because it made me physically move, which helped me wake up for the day

insights.claim: TP-Link Tapo C120 indoor/outdoor security camera at $30 (17% off from $36) was added in the June 25 update—described as offering up to 2K resolution, IP66 weatherproofing, color night vision, smart detection (people, pets, vehicles), and local microSD or cloud storage with a magnetic mount—positioned as WIRED editor Simon Hill's pick.
insights.speaker: Simon Hill
insights.so_what: TP-Link Tapo C120 at $30 with 2K resolution and AI detection competing in the sub-$30 security camera market signals Chinese OEM commoditization of smart home security hardware—a price floor dynamic relevant to Arlo, Google Nest, and Eufy competitive positioning.
insights.quote: Compact and affordable, with an IP66 rating, this security camera can be used indoors or out
